title: Ascogrammitis clathrata
intro: Ascogrammitis clathrata, also known as the lily pad fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The lily pad fern is prized for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Ascogrammitis clathrata include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the lily pad fern is commonly prepared as a tea or infused in water to treat various ailments, although its use is not well-documented in modern scientific literature.
